_comment: Single source of truth for entity pages. Edit items here (or via scripts/add-items.js) and run `node scripts/build.js`. Generated HTML is never edited by hand. 'kind' drives the page template: 'bulletin' pages tie to a (category,country) series in visa-bulletin.json and show a priority-date tool; 'explainer' pages are SEO content + a lead CTA.

employment-based.name: Employment-Based Green Cards
employment-based.blurb: Permanent residence through employment (EB-1 through EB-5).

work-visas.name: Work Visas (Nonimmigrant)
work-visas.blurb: Temporary visas that let you work in the U.S.

items.name: EB-2 India
items.blurb: Employment-based second preference for India-born applicants — one of the most backlogged categories in the Visa Bulletin, with waits commonly measured in years.

items.name: EB-3 India
items.blurb: Employment-based third preference for India-born applicants. EB-3 and EB-2 India cutoffs often cross, which is why some applicants consider an EB-3 downgrade.

items.name: EB-1 India
items.blurb: Employment-based first preference for India-born applicants (extraordinary ability, outstanding researchers, multinational managers).

items.name: EB-2 China
items.blurb: Employment-based second preference for China (mainland-born) applicants — a heavily backlogged category second only to India.

items.name: EB-3 China
items.blurb: Employment-based third preference for China (mainland-born) applicants.

items.name: EB-1 China
items.blurb: Employment-based first preference for China (mainland-born) applicants.

items.name: EB-2 (All Other Countries)
items.blurb: Employment-based second preference for applicants chargeable to all countries other than India, China, Mexico and the Philippines.

items.name: EB-3 (All Other Countries)
items.blurb: Employment-based third preference for applicants chargeable to all countries other than the backlogged ones.

items.name: EB-2 Mexico
items.blurb: Employment-based second preference for Mexico-born applicants.

items.name: EB-2 Philippines
items.blurb: Employment-based second preference for Philippines-born applicants.

items.name: EB-1A Extraordinary Ability
items.blurb: A self-petitioned green card for people at the very top of their field — no employer or labor certification required.

items.name: EB-2 National Interest Waiver (NIW)
items.blurb: An EB-2 green card that waives the job-offer and labor-certification requirement when your work is in the national interest. Popular with researchers, founders and skilled professionals.

items.name: PERM Labor Certification
items.blurb: The Department of Labor step most EB-2 and EB-3 cases start with — it sets your priority date.

items.name: EB-5 Immigrant Investor
items.blurb: A green card through a qualifying U.S. investment that creates jobs. Has its own reserved visa categories and priority-date dynamics.

items.name: H-1B Specialty Occupation Visa
items.blurb: The main employer-sponsored work visa for specialty occupations — allocated by an annual lottery now weighted by wage level.

items.name: O-1 Extraordinary Ability Visa
items.blurb: A work visa for individuals with extraordinary ability or achievement — a common path when the H-1B lottery doesn't come through. No annual cap.

items.name: L-1 Intracompany Transfer Visa
items.blurb: For employees transferring from a foreign office to a related U.S. office. A frequent H-1B alternative for multinational employers.

items.name: TN Visa (USMCA Professionals)
items.blurb: A work visa for Canadian and Mexican professionals under USMCA. No lottery and no annual cap.

items.name: Cap-Exempt H-1B
items.blurb: Universities, nonprofit research organizations and affiliated employers can sponsor H-1Bs outside the lottery, all year round.
